summary: The session focused on calculating the percentage of words in different labels using Python's `CountVectorizer` and addressing a `KeyError` issue when words were not present in all labels. The user requested an automated method to adjust the percentage threshold to ensure at least 100 common words were identified across all labels.
facts: The user encountered a `KeyError` when trying to access a word that was present in one label but not in another. The user requested to auto-tune the percentage threshold to ensure more than 100 common words were identified across all labels. The assistant provided a code snippet that implemented a binary search algorithm to find the appropriate percentage threshold for common words. The user was working with a dataset loaded from "train.csv" containing text data categorized by unique labels.
